education.degree: Bachelor of Science in Real-Time Interactive Simulation (RTIS)

projects.title: 3D Game
bullets: Made a game with a game loop and sprite rendering.
Added mob spawner and enemy AI.
Implemented HP bar and HUD using C++.

projects.title: Multiplayer Game
bullets: Built game loop and tiled map loading.

required_skills: Proficiency in at least one systems programming language: Python, Go, or C++
Experience with Linux operating systems and shell scripting (Bash)
Understanding of networking fundamentals: TCP/IP, DNS, load balancing
Experience with at least one cloud platform: AWS, GCP, or Azure
Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail
Good written and verbal communication in English
preferred_skills: Experience with CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, Jenkins, or equivalent)
Familiarity with Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, Pulumi)
Knowledge of distributed systems and consensus algorithms
Exposure to financial systems or trading platforms
responsibilities: Design and implement RESTful APIs and microservices using Python and Go
Automate infrastructure provisioning with Terraform and Ansible
Monitor system health using Prometheus, Grafana, and ELK stack
Participate in on-call rotation and incident response
Collaborate with software engineers, data engineers, and QA teams

missing.why_it_matters: The role requires proficiency in Python, Go, or C++. Missing these languages limits the candidate's ability to handle core backend systems development.
Experience with Linux and Bash is essential for infrastructure roles. This skill is necessary for automating tasks and managing server environments.
Understanding networking fundamentals is crucial for designing and implementing robust distributed systems and infrastructure.
Cloud platform experience is mandatory for modern systems engineering. This knowledge is needed to deploy and manage scalable infrastructure.
This is a core requirement for any engineering role. The résumé needs to explicitly demonstrate how complex technical challenges were solved.
Effective communication is necessary for writing documentation, collaborating with teams, and running incident response procedures.
Experience with CI/CD is preferred for automating deployment processes. This demonstrates familiarity with modern DevOps practices.
Infrastructure as Code tools are preferred for provisioning infrastructure. Including these shows practical experience in automation.
Observability tools are preferred for monitoring system health. Listing these shows familiarity with modern monitoring practices.

flags.bullet_text: Implemented HP bar and HUD using C++.
Built game loop and tiled map loading.

ats_red_flags.evidence: Projects are listed before Education and Skills. While acceptable for a portfolio/student, standard ATS prefers Education or Experience near the top.

background_fit.candidate_background_summary: The candidate has a background in game development, focusing on C++ and game logic implementation through projects like 3D games and multiplayer netcode.
background_fit.role_requirements_summary: The role requires proficiency in systems programming languages (Python, Go, or C++), Linux scripting, cloud platforms, infrastructure as code, and observability tools.
background_fit.alignment_commentary: While the candidate possesses experience with C++, the résumé profile lacks any demonstrated experience or education in core systems engineering concepts such as Linux, cloud platforms, networking fundamentals, or Infrastructure as Code tools. There is no evidence of experience with the required DevOps or distributed systems buzzwords.

summary:
*   The résumé fails the ATS threshold due to a critical lack of required systems engineering keywords, specifically missing mandatory experience in Python/Go, Linux scripting, cloud platforms (AWS/GCP/Azure), and Infrastructure as Code tools (Terraform/Ansible).
*   Background fit is extremely low; while C++ skills are present, there is no demonstrated experience or education in core infrastructure, networking fundamentals, or modern DevOps practices necessary for the target role.
*   Bullet points lack specificity and measurable impact, resulting in poor quality content that fails to translate project work into relevant systems engineering achievements.
